title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in the Netherlands
description: The Dutch same-sex marriage law came into force on 1 April 2001, making the Netherlands the first country in the world to open civil marriage to same-sex couples. The law also granted joint adoption rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Registered life partnerships take effect in Germany
description: Germany's registered life partnership law came into force, giving same-sex couples a nationwide legal partnership status. Adoption was initially excluded; step-child adoption was added in October 2004.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Finland passes registered partnership law
description: The Finnish parliament passed a registered partnership law for same-sex couples. Adoption was excluded at first; step-child adoption followed in May 2009.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Portugal recognizes same-sex de facto unions
description: Portugal passed and brought into effect a law extending de facto union status to same-sex couples, without joint adoption rights. The framework was superseded by full marriage in 2010.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Canton of Geneva introduces partnership registration
description: A cantonal partnership law for same-sex couples came into effect in the Swiss canton of Geneva, providing limited legal recognition without joint adoption.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rhode Island and Maryland expand anti-discrimination law
description: Rhode Island added gender identity to its private-sector anti-discrimination law, and Maryland added sexual orientation to its private-sector protections.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Albania equalizes the age of consent
description: Albania set an equal age of consent for same-sex and different-sex sexual activity, removing the higher age previously applied to same-sex acts.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Estonia equalizes the age of consent
description: Estonia set an equal age of consent for same-sex and different-sex sexual activity, ending the stricter rule that had applied to same-sex acts.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Liechtenstein equalizes the age of consent
description: Liechtenstein set an equal age of consent for same-sex and different-sex sexual activity, removing the discriminatory higher threshold for same-sex acts.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: United Kingdom equalizes the age of consent
description: The equal age of consent came into force in the United Kingdom, lowering the age for same-sex sexual activity to match that for different-sex activity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Arizona repeals its sodomy law
description: The US state of Arizona repealed its statutes criminalizing consensual same-sex sexual activity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Homosexuality decriminalized in remaining UK territories
description: Consensual same-sex sexual activity was decriminalized in the remaining British overseas territories, completing decriminalization across UK-administered jurisdictions.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: China removes homosexuality from mental disorder classification
description: The third edition of the Chinese Classification of Mental Disorders (CCMD-3) removed homosexuality from its list of mental illnesses.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First Belgrade Pride march attacked
description: The first Pride march in Belgrade was violently disrupted by counter-protesters, with participants beaten. No further Pride was attempted in Serbia until years later.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First US memorial to LGBT veterans dedicated
description: A memorial honoring LGBT military veterans was dedicated in Desert Memorial Park in Cathedral City, California — the first such memorial in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First two women in the world legally marry
description: Helene Faasen and Anne-Marie Thus married in Amsterdam under the new Dutch marriage law, becoming the first two women anywhere to enter a legal same-sex marriage.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Pink Triangle Park dedicated in San Francisco
description: Pink Triangle Park was dedicated in San Francisco as the first permanent free-standing memorial in the United States to the homosexual victims persecuted in Nazi Germany.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Quebec introduces civil unions with adoption rights
description: The Canadian province of Quebec passed and brought into force a civil union law for same-sex couples that included joint adoption rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Finland's registered partnership law takes effect
description: Finland's registered partnership law for same-sex couples came into force. Joint adoption remained excluded; step-child adoption followed in May 2009.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Buenos Aires passes civil union law
description: The city of Buenos Aires passed a civil union law for same-sex couples, the first legislation of its kind in Latin America. It did not include joint adoption.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Canton of Zurich approves partnership law
description: The Swiss canton of Zurich approved a cantonal partnership law for same-sex couples, without joint adoption rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: South Africa opens adoption to same-sex couples
description: Same-sex couples in South Africa gained the right to joint and step-child adoption.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sweden opens adoption to same-sex couples
description: Sweden granted registered same-sex partners the right to joint and step-child adoption.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Alaska and New York add sexual orientation protections
description: Alaska barred sexual-orientation discrimination in the public sector, and New York State enacted private-sector protections covering sexual orientation.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Northwest Territories bans gender identity discrimination
description: Canada's Northwest Territories updated its human rights legislation to explicitly include sexual orientation and became the first Canadian jurisdiction to prohibit discrimination based on gender identity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Austria equalizes the age of consent
description: Austria removed the higher age of consent that had applied to sexual activity between men, equalizing it with the general age of consent.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Bulgaria equalizes the age of consent
description: Bulgaria set an equal age of consent for same-sex and different-sex sexual activity, removing the higher threshold for same-sex acts.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Cyprus equalizes the age of consent
description: Cyprus set an equal age of consent for same-sex and different-sex sexual activity, ending the discriminatory higher age for same-sex acts.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Hungary equalizes the age of consent
description: Hungary equalized the age of consent for same-sex and different-sex sexual activity after its Constitutional Court struck down the unequal rule.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Moldova equalizes the age of consent
description: Moldova set an equal age of consent for same-sex and different-sex sexual activity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Romania equalizes the age of consent
description: Romania set an equal age of consent for same-sex and different-sex sexual activity as part of the reform that repealed its anti-gay penal provisions.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Western Australia equalizes the age of consent
description: The Australian state of Western Australia set an equal age of consent for same-sex and different-sex sexual activity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Romania's repeal of Article 200 takes effect
description: The repeal of Article 200 of Romania's penal code took effect, ending the criminalization of consensual same-sex relations.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Costa Rica repeals sodomy provision
description: Costa Rica repealed its remaining sodomy provision, removing criminal penalties targeting consensual same-sex activity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Arkansas and Massachusetts sodomy laws struck down
description: Court rulings invalidated the sodomy laws of the US states of Arkansas and Massachusetts, ending criminal penalties for consensual same-sex activity in both states.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Openly gay politician Pim Fortuyn assassinated
description: Dutch politician Pim Fortuyn, openly gay and leading his own party into national elections, was shot dead by Volkert van der Graaf in Hilversum shortly before the general election.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: PFLAG establishes its Transgender Network
description: Parents and Friends of Lesbians and Gays made its Transgender Network (TNET) the organization's first official Special Affiliate, with the same standing as its regular chapters.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First rabbinical school seminar on trans and intersex issues
description: Rabbi Margaret Wenig organized the first school-wide seminar at any rabbinical school on the psychological, legal, and religious issues affecting intersex and transsexual people, held at Hebrew Union College-Jewish Institute of Religion in New York.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Belgium legalizes same-sex marriage
description: Belgium passed and brought into force a law opening civil marriage to same-sex couples, becoming the second country in the world to do so. Joint adoption was excluded until April 2006.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ontario and British Columbia begin same-sex marriages
description: Court rulings made Ontario and British Columbia the first Canadian provinces where same-sex couples could legally marry.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Buenos Aires civil union law takes effect
description: The civil union law of the city of Buenos Aires came into force, allowing same-sex couples to register the first legally recognized unions in Latin America.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tasmania passes relationship registration law
description: The Australian state of Tasmania passed legislation creating registered partnerships for same-sex couples, with step-child adoption only.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Austria grants limited recognition to same-sex cohabitation
description: Limited legal recognition of same-sex cohabitation came into effect in Austria, without joint adoption; it was superseded by registered partnerships in 2010.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Croatia enacts law on same-sex unions
description: Croatia's law on same-sex civil unions came into effect, granting limited rights to cohabiting same-sex couples without registration or adoption.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Bulgaria bans discrimination based on sexual orientation
description: Bulgaria adopted anti-discrimination legislation covering sexual orientation across all sectors.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: UK outlaws sexual orientation discrimination at work
description: The United Kingdom introduced regulations banning employment discrimination based on sexual orientation, with an exemption for religious organisations.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Five US states expand anti-discrimination protections
description: Arizona, Kentucky, Michigan, New Mexico and Pennsylvania added protections covering sexual orientation and in some cases gender identity, in scopes ranging from public-sector employment to full private-sector coverage.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Russia ends ban on gay people in the military
description: Russia's formal ban on gay people serving in the armed forces ended, although restrictions tied to medical and psychiatric assessments persisted in practice.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New South Wales and Northern Territory equalize age of consent
description: The Australian state of New South Wales and the Northern Territory set equal ages of consent for same-sex and different-sex sexual activity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Armenia decriminalizes same-sex relations
description: Armenia's new criminal code came into force without the Soviet-era article criminalizing sexual relations between men, decriminalizing consensual same-sex activity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: United Kingdom abolishes buggery as a legal concept
description: UK sexual offences reform removed buggery as a distinct criminal offence, eliminating the last statutory remnant of the historic criminalization of same-sex acts.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lawrence v. Texas strikes down US sodomy laws
description: In Lawrence v. Texas the US Supreme Court ruled that laws criminalizing consensual same-sex intimacy are unconstitutional, invalidating the remaining sodomy laws nationwide.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Homosexuality decriminalized in Iraq
description: With the restoration of Iraq's 1969 penal code after the 2003 invasion, consensual same-sex relations were no longer criminalized under national law.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Belize recriminalizes same-sex relations
description: Belize was reported to have recriminalized consensual same-sex sexual activity; Section 53 of its criminal code continued to penalize such acts until it was struck down in 2016.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Section 28 repealed in England, Wales and Northern Ireland
description: The repeal of Section 28, which had barred local authorities from 'promoting homosexuality', took effect in England, Wales and Northern Ireland; Scotland had repealed its equivalent in 2000.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Gene Robinson becomes first openly gay Episcopal bishop
description: Gene Robinson was consecrated bishop of New Hampshire, becoming the first openly gay bishop in the Episcopal Church in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Reuben Zellman admitted to Reform rabbinical school
description: Reuben Zellman became the first openly transgender person accepted to Hebrew Union College-Jewish Institute of Religion; he was ordained in 2010.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Conservative Judaism recognizes sex reassignment in Jewish law
description: The Committee on Jewish Law and Standards approved a ruling that sex reassignment surgery is a permissible treatment for gender dysphoria and that it changes a transgender person's sex status under Jewish law.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Australia issues first documents showing indeterminate gender
description: Alex MacFarlane became the first person reported to obtain a birth certificate and passport showing indeterminate gender, issued in Australia.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Trans and intersex seminar held at Reconstructionist Rabbinical College
description: Rabbi Margaret Wenig organized the first school-wide seminar at the Reconstructionist Rabbinical College addressing the psychological, legal, and religious issues affecting intersex and transsexual people.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jennifer Finney Boylan's memoir becomes a bestseller
description: She's Not There: A Life in Two Genders by Jennifer Finney Boylan became the first bestselling book by an openly transgender American author.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer airs pioneering lesbian bedroom scene
description: Buffy the Vampire Slayer showed girlfriends Willow Rosenberg and Tara Maclay in bed together, regarded as the first scene of its kind on a US broadcast network series.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First lesbian sex scene airs on US broadcast television
description: An episode of Buffy the Vampire Slayer included what is considered the first lesbian sex scene in US broadcast television history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Patrick Harvie becomes first openly bisexual MSP
description: Patrick Harvie was elected to the Scottish Parliament, becoming its first openly bisexual member.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage reaches six more Canadian jurisdictions
description: Legal same-sex marriage came to the Canadian provinces of Manitoba, Newfoundland and Labrador, Nova Scotia, Quebec and Saskatchewan, and to the Yukon territory.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Massachusetts becomes first US state with same-sex marriage
description: Following the Goodridge v. Department of Public Health ruling, Massachusetts began issuing marriage licenses to same-sex couples, the first US state to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rio Grande do Sul recognizes same-sex civil unions
description: The Brazilian state of Rio Grande do Sul passed and brought into effect recognition of civil unions for same-sex couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Luxembourg enacts registered partnership law
description: Luxembourg's registered partnership law took effect, giving same-sex couples legal recognition without joint adoption rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Maine enacts domestic partnership law
description: The US state of Maine passed and brought into effect a domestic partnership law providing limited legal recognition for same-sex couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tasmania's relationship registration takes effect
description: Tasmania's registered partnership scheme for same-sex couples came into force, with step-child adoption only.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
